notes: The Winetricks corefonts verb is required to make the Rockstar Social Club launcher display text properly. The game itself crashed for me (https://pastebin.com/su4z7Pw7), regardless of whether I had the game's built in safe mode, DXVK, and/or esync enabled.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes: Social club launcher opens, uses a very weird monospace font (Is corefonts installed with SteamPlay?) and Social club launcher loads seemingly forever. GTA V doesn't open, but there are no errors.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
- Does not run without winetricks corefonts and even then issues with starting may occur, there is a problem with the social club login screen that causes the login field and buttons to disappear, a work around is use lutris for the first boot, run the GTA V install script for lutris, when steam is installed and logged in don't install GTA V, go to your steam settings, go to downloads and add a library pointing to your steam library (the folder that contains the steamapps folder with your GTA V install in it), it may need to update, then launch the game, log into social club, get to the main menu, and close the game, switch back to steam play and you should log in automatically before the login fields disappear.

- Use the PROTON_NO_ESYNC launch parameter to avoid low framerates

- Game runs mostly fine with minor stuttering, Online seems to work fine, have not tested being in large gunfights. Some cutscenes lack audio and are a little choppy. Overall the game is playable but not perfect.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes: Works with way less cursor lag in menus than my lutris/dxvk setup. You need to follow the workaround here: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/37#issuecomment-415833819

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
custom dxvk config with "dxgi.customDeviceId = E366
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002" in launch options DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/gta_dxvk.conf %command%

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
To fix the Social Club launcher issue (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/37#issuecomment-415833819): 

Create a file named gta_dxvk.conf with this:

dxgi.customDeviceId = E366
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002

set this launch options:

DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/gta_dxvk.conf %command%

title: Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion
notes: Sound for voices got a little scratchy near the end of a match.

Needs launch options "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/my_dxvk.conf %command%". Your config file should have 2 lines "
dxgi.customDeviceId = E366 
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002
".

It's playable, but super low framerate about 15fps or less on 1080p with normal settings, this is probably due to my CPU, an 15 with only 4 cores. Mouse has issues, it sometimes gets stuck and you can't move the camera. Xbox 360 controller works fine with dumbxinput.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes: Tested with NixOS. Needed to set `DefaultLimitNOFILE=1024:1048576`  for systemd system/user config even with Esync disabled. Minor audio crackling. Game was much smoother using Fullscreen instead of Borderless Window (average FPS went from 77 to 92, Borderless Window stuttered frequently)
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
Game works very well with some minor issues such as mouse lock-up (while driving a car) and VSync does not limit FPS, but everything else is great.

At first I had infinite loading screen to story mode and ERR_AUD_INIT_1

My solution was here https://github.com/lutris/lutris/wiki/How-to:-Esync

I use Gentoo Linux and it seems they have limit of 4096 for DefaultLimitNOFILE which after changing to 1048576 has solved everything. You can check on your system value of this limit by running ulimit -Hn visit the link to Lutris Wiki for more info how to solve this ;)

notes: Works great, I had a issue with social club crashing/not appearing, applying these startup parameters fixed it and the games works great. WINEDLLOVERRIDES="msdmo=b" %command%
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
Works perfectly both story mode and on line.
I followed the help of other users using these instructions:

Created a gta_dxvk.conf file with this text
dxgi.customDeviceId = E366
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002

Used that file location in the games launch options (right click on game in steam client, select "properties" then "Launch Options"

DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/home/trax/gta_dxvk.conf %command%  ou

DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/home/trax/gta_dxvk.conf PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%


Renamed file "user_settings.sample.py" in folder "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.7/" to "user_settings.py", Edited the settings file and uncommented

"PROTON_NO_ESYNC": "1",

Optional: I enabled DKVK's HUD in this same file

"DXVK_HUD": "devinfo,fps"

notes:
I played a lot and found the graphics features that slows down the performance very significantly compared to playing on Windows:

-- NVIDIA PCSS soft shadows. Causes severe slowdowns when going near trees.

-- Ultra reflections. Drops FPS severely.

Other settings can be set to ultra values without a problem.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
The game worked perfect on my desktop out of the box, online works too. The only issues I encountered was with the sound where after a few hours of play you may get crackling noises, restarting the game fixes it. The mouse can also be a bit wonky, changing it to direct input mode instead of raw input seemed to help. The performance is stellar, very close to native.

On my laptop with a 950m, the game works just as well but I had to use nvidia-xrun (the game would not launch properly with primusrun, gave me a dx10/11 error, this is a common issue for nvidia optimus laptops)
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes: Played Story mode. Great performance. Very stable.
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
When I started the game for the first time, I receive an error "The Social Club failed to load due to an incomplete installation (code 9) please exit the game and re-install the latest version of the Social Club". Re-installation of GTA V from Steam didn't help.

Found that it was due to a missing "My Documents" folder. Managed to fix it by adding the folder into "SteamLibrary/SteamApps/compatdata/271590/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser".

May need to reverify integrity of the game files. Right click on GTA V in steam, select properties, local files, verify. This seems to trigger a re-installation of Social Club. Not sure if this step is really necessary.
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
'SELF-RADIO' doesn't work, audio crackles after several hours of play (reset fixes this) otherwise both singleplayer and Online seem to work very well.

(Would be interesting to see if others have the same issue with 'Self-Radio' not working).
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
Ran perfectly upon installation - I already had mscorefonts installed and did not need to disable esync.

One problem I ran in to recently was the game stopped loading after my driver was updated from 415.13 to 415.18, I dialog window popped up to say I didn't have a DirectX compatible graphics card. Changing to a previous driver (NVIDIA 410), restarting, then back to 415.18, and restarting again seemed to do the trick.

I also cannot go to the Activities Overview whilst in-game as I get a ERR_GFX_D3D-INIT 'Failed initialization' warning however this is by no means a huge concern or a deal breaker.
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes: Tried first running with Nvidia 396 provided with Debian Buster. The game ran but with lots of rendering artifacts, making it actually unplayable. Then installed latest Nvidia stable driver manually 410.78 : sometimes the game crashes at startup. sometimes the game has no sound. Previous rendering artifacts have disappeared, but new artifacts appeared (textures popping up suddenly). Sound in the intro screen, but no more sound in the game. Not able to finish even the first mission due to NPC stalled. This is far, far from playable.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
I've tried all of the tweaks, tried this guide: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1581032383 with the most updated beta drivers, as well as the NVIDIA drivers from my repo. 

The launcher lets me log in, with "Disable Esync" tweak, but will crash after and let me retry or retry in safe mode. Neither option works. With either the tweak for Disable D3d11 and using Wine D3d11, the launcher will not log in, but infinitely load. Adding "-scOfflineOnly" to the launch options skipped the launcher, but the game crashed immediately.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
In order to get through the Social Club DRM, you need to have mscorefonts installed. The installation process varies from distro to distro, so be sure to look into that yourself.

THE GOOD NEWS: The game runs, very well I might add. Performance is right alongside how it runs on Windows. The DRM functions without issue with mscorefonts, and I can play both in single player and online mode without a hitch. The game is fully playable, and runs very well.

THE BAD NEWS: Random crashes every 30 minutes to an hour. It's probably trying to access some dll file that doesn't exist or somesuch, but either way, it breaks frequently. Happens with or without Esync. With this game's long loading times, frequent crashes are well... not good.

If you can handle frequent crashes, I'd say go for it. The game runs very nicely when it's not randomly broken, but that's the qualifier.

Tested on both Proton 3.16-4 and 3.7-8.
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
The game runs perfectly, the only thing you have to do(manually)is to create a folder called "My Documents" inside ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/271590/pfx/drive_c/users/steamusers. This will solve the Social Club error(which say: The social club failed to load due to an incomplete installation(code 9)...), after that a file integrity check is needed to force steam to reinstall Social Club client. 
Apart of that the game runs very smootly(~60fps) without any kind of freeze, glitch or crash and online works very well too.
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
I followed this redditor's solution. The customDeviceId and customVendorId fix was necessary, otherwise the game consistently kept crashing to desktop after about 15-20 mins of play.
I do still observe this weird behaviour where, when you open the main menu while in-game, the framerate gradually drops to the low 10s. Goes away as you close the menu and continue playing. Gameplay itself seems smooth.

https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/99ikj4/gta_v_is_running_pretty_well_for_me_in_proton/e5d5191/
title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
I installed the mscorefonts with the ttf-ms-win10 aur package (make sure to read the PKGBUILD) and rebooted.

I also applied the dxvk fix from here : https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/37#issuecomment-41583381

So the entire commandline options are like this  :
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/home/users/Documents/gta_dxvk.conf %command%

Then everything works, no issues.

notes: I was impressed on how it was seamless. Completely "Install and play" experience with my configs.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

cpu: AMD A10-5800K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes:
I have played from the beginning, to where Michael DeSanta defeats the police after getting knocked out, by Dave the FIB matey who helped Michael fake his death. I am so pleased this game works, nearly out of the bag. Peppermint OS sorted out the files needed for this game to work, My Documents, My Music, My Videos, My Downloads and other files, were in the right place for Social Club to activate straight away. 

If you're having trouble with the error 9 from Social Club add My Documents to home folder open hidden files, with Ctrl+H or alternatively, left click View at the top of your homefolder menu and tick the box that says Show Hidden Files. 
Next double click .steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/Numberedfile/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser. 

In there right click empty space and add a new folder, name it and make sure you spell it correctly, name it My Documents. Before launching this game, go to your game library right click GTA 5 in your game list, left click at the bottom properties and left click Set Launch Options, in the box that comes up enter this code PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% This command disables ESYNC in the game. If you are going to use the 360 wired controller, I suggest unticking  the guide  focuses steam box in settings/controller/General Controller Settings with your 360 controller plugged in, also at the bottom of the list, set timeout of your 360 controller to never. On the first mission of this game, where the controls gets handed over to Trevor to take the guard out, for some odd reason the controller does not work properly, so push F5 to take control, then you can use your wired 360 controller to shoot the guard and throughout the rest of this fantastic game.  Other than these few tweaks, the game works flawlessly. The sound after ten or so hours non stop, does crackle a bit, but a restart of the game sorts it out. Thank you Proton Developers, for making my dreams of playing this brilliant game on my Linux Machine a reality.

title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
This report leaves out all the modifications made to Solus, like NOFILE descriptor count and Budgie G Sync fixes as i made them before the game was finished downloading.

I first started GTA with "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" which worked perfectly for 20min+. I then removed that launch option and it sill works like a charm,  and even yields more frames in "low work" areas, like save houses.

I had some problems with GTA not saving settings and being confused about what the current settings are. Every time I started GTA I was asked to set the gamma and in the settings it would show 800x600 despite the game running in 1080p windowed. This issue disappeared after some starts.
Also SocialClub reported an 1000.50 error which was resolved by restarting Steam.

While playing the game, it stutters quite hard when it has to load new assets (from SSD) which I'm not used to on Windows.

Online seems to work well, too. I could join a friends only lobby first try.
No crashes so far. (It might even load faster than on windows, story and online)

title: Grand Theft Auto V

notes:
I think many testers have not tried to play this for a long time.
Single player: The rendering just stops after random times, usually about 30 minutes of play. The game has to be killed and restarted. Whatever mission you were on has to be restarted.
Saves sometimes fail. You sometimes need to go and reset the permissions on the files in ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/271590/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/Rockstar Games/GTA V/ which seems to temporarily fix the save failed issue in single player.


Multiplayer: After some time the game stops communicating with the rockstar servers for downloading mission data and saving your progress. This is a well known issues even windows users have sometimes. But through proton, this happens every time you play. For example, after going online, you can play maybe 2 heist setup missions, but by the third or fourth the game will start giving you errors and not save your progress. If you continue ignoring these errors, the game crashes sometime afterward.

Anything which requires text entry such as naming saved outfits results in an error message about invalid characters. LC_ALL=C seems to have fixed this issue for me.

While trying to modify graphics options, the controls seem to be repeated. Sometimes it will tell you you are exiting the menu when you did not intend to. The only way to actually save your changes is to hit esc, allowing the game to give you the warning, and then hit enter. But you must change something that requires a restart. If the game does not restart at that point, no graphics options will actually be changed.

DOF does not work.

I'm not seeing any difference with or without PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 .

DX11 does appear to be working. All of the above still happens with DX10.
title: Grand Theft Auto V
Grand Theft Auto V
notes: The Winetricks corefonts verb is required to make the Rockstar Social Club launcher display text properly. The game itself crashed for me (https://pastebin.com/su4z7Pw7), regardless of whether I had the game's built in safe mode, DXVK, and/or esync enabled.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
- Does not run without winetricks corefonts and even then issues with starting may occur, there is a problem with the social club login screen that causes the login field and buttons to disappear, a work around is use lutris for the first boot, run the GTA V install script for lutris, when steam is installed and logged in don't install GTA V, go to your steam settings, go to downloads and add a library pointing to your steam library (the folder that contains the steamapps folder with your GTA V install in it), it may need to update, then launch the game, log into social club, get to the main menu, and close the game, switch back to steam play and you should log in automatically before the login fields disappear.

- Use the PROTON_NO_ESYNC launch parameter to avoid low framerates

- Game runs mostly fine with minor stuttering, Online seems to work fine, have not tested being in large gunfights. Some cutscenes lack audio and are a little choppy. Overall the game is playable but not perfect.

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes: Works with way less cursor lag in menus than my lutris/dxvk setup. You need to follow the workaround here: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/37#issuecomment-415833819

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
custom dxvk config with "dxgi.customDeviceId = E366
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002" in launch options DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/gta_dxvk.conf %command%

title: Grand Theft Auto V
notes:
To fix the Social Club launcher issue (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/37#issuecomment-415833819): 

Create a file named gta_dxvk.conf with this:

dxgi.customDeviceId = E366
dxgi.customVendorId = 1002

set this launch options:

DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/path/to/gta_dxvk.conf %command%
